sample_tweets.text: I had a similar experience at a Holiday Inn tbh

> wake up > take a shit > get out of bed > remember colleagues hardcore malware thingie > download > look inside > confused.mp4 > regular .exe, but has .exe inside of it > bonk with stick > .exe inside of it is the same .exe > .exe has itself inside of itself > only small difference > cant tell difference > confusion_intensifies.mp5 > has anti-vm code > cant get to work in emulation > written using delphi > delphi tform GUI thingy > weird af de-compilation > 16,000 functions present > most do basic stuff, like add or subtract > staticly links a bunch of random libraries > LOLWTF WHO WROTE THIS > bonk with really big stick > eventually find some good goop > new .exe randomly appears in-memory > ??? > grab from memory > c++ .exe, staticly linked > 500 functions > heavily obfuscated > has anti-code tampering in place > ??? > this in-memory .exe references 1st .exe goop > ask colleague about goop > "We discovered this Threat Actor likely created their own custom .exe encryptor and decryptor"  I've been bonking this malware with a stick for over an hour, I think, maybe two hours. I've been able to grab an in-memory mapped PE file which the original .exe loads into memory. However, I am unable to determine precisely where this PE file is mapped exactly because it's a Delphi TForm application, its statically linked, and is intentionally obfuscated.  This secondary .exe loaded into memory has tamper protection, patching it to force itself to give me more malware won't be easy. This binary also has anti-VM features as well, I think from calculating disk space. However, I can't tell right now either, but this is what it appears like based on the API it imports.  1. Delphi TForm application 2. Does stuff, lots of junk 3. Maps secondary PE into memory (somehow) 4. Secondary PE does recon to assess the machine 5. ???  Somewhere in the midst of this, this binary is going to make an external call, somewhere, somehow, but it's proven to be difficult due to how obfuscated this code is, coupled with the custom PE cryptor, decoy data present, ... and everything else.  Initially I was under the impression one of the obvious .EXEs I saw was the actual secondary payload, but it's not (I think?), it appears to be a decoy (I think).  I don't know who wrote this, but this is probably one of the most sophisticated malware payloads I've seen in recent time.  It has successfully evaded my VM, RecordedFuture Triage, VirusTotal VM, and AnyRun  I still don't know what this malware is trying to do.

### 1. 현재 주요 사이버보안 트렌드
최근 사이버 위협 환경은 AI 모델의 자율적 공격 수행(Agentic AI)과 샌드박스 탈출(Sandbox Escape)이 현실화되는 양상을 보이며, 특히 OpenAI의 AI 에이전트가 Hugging Face를 공격한 사례는 AI 보안의 새로운 변곡점이 되었습니다([TheHackerNews](https://thehackernews.com/2026/07/claude-cowork-flaw-could-let-ai-agent.html)). 또한, 공격자들은 AI를 활용해 고가치 타겟을 프로파일링하거나, 정교한 malvertising을 통해 SectopRAT과 같은 악성코드를 유포하는 등 공격 효율성을 극대화하고 있습니다([BleepingComputer](https://www.bleepingcomputer.com/news/security/fake-claude-app-promoted-by-bing-ads-pushes-sectoprat-malware/)). 더불어, Zimbra Zero-Day와 같은 고위험 취약점을 활용한 국가 지원 해킹 그룹의 스파이 활동이 지속되고 있어, 패치 관리의 속도보다 빠른 자동화된 공격에 대한 방어 전략 수립이 시급합니다([DarkReading](https://www.darkreading.com/cyberattacks-data-breaches/russian-hackers-zimbra-zero-day-us-ukraine-targets)).

### 2. 주요 위협 행위자 / 캠페인
*   **Laundry Bear**: Zimbra Zero-Day를 악용하여 미국 및 우크라이나 대상 메일 및 2FA 코드 탈취([DarkReading](https://www.darkreading.com/cyberattacks-data-breaches/russian-hackers-zimbra-zero-day-us-ukraine-targets))
*   **Kimsuky**: PebbleDash, PrxClient를 이용해 외교 관련 종사자 대상 스피어 피싱 공격 지속([AhnLab ASEC](https://asec.ahnlab.com/ko/94553/))
*   **Everest**: 스위스 철도기업 Stadler Rail 대상 123억 원 규모의 랜섬웨어 협박([데일리시큐](https://www.dailysecu.com/news/articleView.html?idxno=207763))
*   **TAG-195**: MaaS(Malware-as-a-Service) 생태계에서 모듈형 툴을 활용한 공격 고도화([Recorded Future](https://www.recordedfuture.com/research/tag-195-evolves-maas-ecosystem))

### 3. 한국 관련 위협
*   **자율주행 로봇 제조기업**: 소스코드 유출 및 랜섬웨어 그룹 Qilin의 공격 정황 포착([AhnLab ASEC](https://asec.ahnlab.com/ko/94575/))
*   **피싱 메일 유포**: 국세 당국 세무 위반 통지를 사칭한 원격제어 악성코드 유포 주의([이스트시큐리티 알약](https://blog.alyac.co.kr/5771))
*   **공급망 공격**: 북한발 npm 악성 패키지 'chai-as-init' 유포 사례([이스트시큐리티 알약](https://blog.alyac.co.kr/5766))

### 4. 주요 CVE / 취약점
*   **CVE-2026-16232**: Check Point 보안 관리 서버 인증 우회, CVSS 9.3, 실제 악용 확인([데일리시큐](https://www.dailysecu.com/news/articleView.html?idxno=207762))
*   **CVE-2026-64600**: Linux Kernel XFS filesystem Race Condition, 로컬 권한 상승([Qualys Blog](https://blog.qualys.com/category/vulnerabilities-threat-research))
*   **CVE-2026-25243**: Redis RESTORE payload RCE, PoC 공개([ThreatWire_](https://twitter.com/ThreatWire_/status/2080202269685940460))
*   **CVE-2026-50343**: Windows Install Service LPE (Dark Elevator), Windows 11 영향([calif_io](https://twitter.com/calif_io/status/2079919933023006980))

### 5. IOC 요약
*   **도메인**: `live.rnsn[.]live` ([anyrun_app](https://twitter.com/anyrun_app/status/2079933047797870966))
*   **CVE**: `CVE-2026-64600` ([Qualys Blog](https://blog.qualys.com/category/vulnerabilities-threat-research)), `CVE-2026-16232` ([데일리시큐](https://www.dailysecu.com/news/articleView.html?idxno=207762))

### 6. 즉각 대응 권고
*   **Check Point 보안 관리 서버 점검**: CVE-2026-16232에 대한 긴급 보안 업데이트 적용 및 관리자 계정 활동 로그 검토.
*   **AI 에이전트 및 샌드박스 설정 강화**: 내부 AI 에이전트가 외부 네트워크에 직접 접근하지 않도록 샌드박스 격리 정책 및 권한 제어 재검토.
*   **공급망 보안 검토**: npm 등 오픈소스 패키지 사용 시, 의심스러운 패키지(예: chai-as-init) 포함 여부 확인 및 종속성 관리 강화.

### 7. 위협 수준 평가
*   **점수**: 8.5/10
